zh.lifeSummary: 當William "the Carpenter" Bisset於1106年出生在英國越可爾霍爾德內斯時，他的父親Sir Henry de Bisset26歲，母親Bertha de Bridgeford22歲。他於1128年與Hawise在英國英格蘭越可爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1186年8月6日在英國諾廷加姆爾賓厄姆三世享年80歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William "the Carpenter" Bisset于1106年出生在英国越可爾霍爾德內斯时，他的父亲Sir Henry de Bisset26岁，母亲Bertha de Bridgeford22岁。他于1128年与Hawise在英国英格兰越可爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1186年8月6日在英国諾廷加姆爾賓厄姆三世享年80岁。

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Memoli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Ciro, Francesco, Luciano, Natale, Nino.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of a pet form of thepersonal name Guglielmo, Italian form of William .

variants.name: McElligott
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brigid, Brendan, Seamus.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uileagóid, a patronymic from a pet form of the Gaelic form of the personal name William .

variants.name: McWilliam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scottish Iain, Morag.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , also Anglicized as Williamson in Scotland. The name is thought to be mainly Scottish in Ulster, though there has been some confusion with McQuillan in County Down. In Connacht, McWilliam was a branch of Burke .

variants.name: Wilcock
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Wilcoc, a pet form of William (composed of the elements Will 1 + the hypocoristic suffix -cok; see Cocke ).

variants.name: McWilliams
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , with excrescent English -s. In Ireland it sometimes represents a partial Gaelicization of Welsh Williams .

variants.name: Lemmo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Donato, Gino.
Italian (southern): from the personal name Lemmo, a pet form of Guglielmo, an equivalent of William . Compare Lembo .

variants.name: Wilk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Mariusz, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Zygmunt, Andrzej, Bogdan, Boguslaw, Casimir, Krzysztof, Stanislawa, Boleslaw, Darek.

Polish: from the vocabulary word wilk ‘wolf’, probably applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble a wolf or connected with wolves in some way. Alternatively, from an Old Slavic personal name based on vlъkъ ‘wolf’.
English (Staffordshire): from Middle English Wilke, a pet form of William and probably a shortening of Wilkin . The usage was very likely Flemish in origin.

variants.name: Wilkin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Wilkin, a pet form of William introduced to England and Scotland by Flemings and Picards after the Norman Conquest.
North German: variant of   Wilken  .

variants.name: Gillam
meanings: English: variant of   Gilliam  .
Welsh: Anglicized form of   Gwilliam  .

variants.name: Lembo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Carmelo, Domenic, Pietro, Alfio, Amelio, Antonio, Biagio, Dante, Giovani, Giovanni.
Italian (southern): from a hypercorrected form of Lemmo, a pet form of Guglièlmo, an equivalent of William .

nameMeanings.name: "the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vietnamese: unexplained.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄭, possibly based on its Hokkien or Teochew pronunciation, see   Zheng   1.

variants.name: Zheng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jian, Yi, Bin, Hong, Hua, Li, Wei, Yong, Ting, Xin, Yan, Hui, Min, Hao, Shen, Da, Quan, Tian, Chong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 鄭: from Zheng (鄭), the name of a state (located in part of present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) granted to You, the younger brother of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). The state of Zheng was annexed by the state of Han (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Carpenter"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): occupational name for a worker in wood, Norman French carpentier (from Late Latin carpentarius ‘cartwright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Zimmermann  , French   Charpentier  , Italian   Carpentieri  , or cognates and equivalents in various other languages.

variants.name: Zimmermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Hans, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Klaus, Gerd, Manfred, Wolfgang, Bernhard.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a carpenter, Middle High German zimberman (a compound of zimber, zim(m)er ‘timber, wood’ + man ‘man’), German Zimmermann.

nameMeanings.name: Bisset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Bissett  .
French: from a diminutive of Old French bisse ‘fine linen’ (see Bisson ).

variants.name: Bissett
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French biset ‘dark’, a diminutive of bis (see Biss ).

Altered form of French Bizet: nickname for someone with a dark complexion, derived from bis ‘grayish-brown’. Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t. Compare Bissette .

History: The surname Bissett (see 1 above) was brought to Scotland from Nottinghamshire c. 1175 by Henricus Byset, an Englishman who became a retainer of King William the Lion. — It was Jean George Bizet from Haute-Saône, France, whose surname was changed to Bissett (see 2 above) after his immigration to NS, Canada, in 1752.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William "the Carpenter" Bisset was born in 1106, in Holderness, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Henry de Bisset, was 26 and his mother, Bertha de Bridgeford, was 22. He married Hawise in 1128, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 6 August 1186, in Bingham, Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William "the Carpenter" Bisset est né en 1106, à Holderness, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Henry de Bisset, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Bertha de Bridgeford, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Hawise en 1128, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 6 août 1186, à Bingham, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 80 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William "the Carpenter" Bisset 1106 in Holderness, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Henry de Bisset 26 und seine Mutter Bertha de Bridgeford 22. Er hat 1128 in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Hawise geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb am 6. August 1186 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Bingham, Nottinghamshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William "the Carpenter" Bisset è nato il nel 1106 nel Holderness, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Henry de Bisset, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Bertha de Bridgeford, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Hawise nel 1128, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 6 agosto 1186, nel Bingham, Nottinghamshire, England, all'età di 80 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William "the Carpenter" Bissetが1106年にイギリスヨークシュアイアホールデルネスで生まれた時，父Sir Henry de Bissetは26歳，母Bertha de Bridgefordは22歳であった。1128年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアでHawiseと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1186年8月6日にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアビンガムで80歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William "the Carpenter" Bisset(이)가 1106년 영국 요르크실르 홀더네스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Henry de Bisset(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Bertha de Bridgeford(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1128년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 Hawise(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1186년 8월 6일 영국 노틴그함실르 빙함에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William "the Carpenter" Bisset nasceu em 1106, em Holderness, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Henry de Bisset, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Bertha de Bridgeford, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Hawise em 1128, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 6 de agosto de 1186, em Bingham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 80 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William "the Carpenter" Bisset родился в 1106 г. в местности холдирнесс, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Henry de Bisset было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Bertha de Bridgeford, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Hawise в 1128 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 6 августа 1186 г. в местности бингхам, ноттингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 80 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William "the Carpenter" Bisset nació en 1106, en Holderness, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Henry de Bisset, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Bertha de Bridgeford, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Hawise en 1128, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 6 de agosto de 1186, en Bingham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 80 años.
